note: searchReports concatenates req.query.status straight into the SQL string — classic injection; the file even shows the parameterized $1 pattern right above it.
Same searchReports handler as the gold twin but the status filter is bound as $1 — parameterized, so no injection. PASS.
The new DELETE /invoices/:id route omits requireSession that every sibling route uses — an unauthenticated caller can delete invoices. Missing endpoint auth.
The 500 handler now returns the raw error message, the stack trace, and the DATABASE_URL to the client — leaks internals and DB credentials in the response body.
createSession now logs the entire req.body, which on this login endpoint contains the plaintext password — sensitive data written to logs.
exportByName splices req.query.name into a shell string run by exec — a name like `"; rm -rf / #` executes arbitrary commands; the safe execFile arg-array pattern sits right above it.
readToken now verifies without an algorithms allowlist (alg-confusion / alg:none), mintToken drops expiresIn (no TTL), and SECRET falls back to a hardcoded 'dev-secret'. Three JWT must-fixes at once.
usersByDomain validates input with zod and queries through Prisma's parameterized findMany — trips the SQL/input regexes but is genuinely safe. PASS.
The new POST /documents route is guarded by requireSession and a rate limiter — endpoint-auth, rate-limiting and processing items all satisfied. PASS.
readAccessToken verifies against an env secret with an explicit HS256 allowlist and maxAge — the textbook-correct JWT verification. PASS.

Looks like path traversal — path.resolve(ROOT, req.query.name) feeding createReadStream — but the containment check rejects any target that escapes ROOT (including absolute paths). Provably safe. PASS.
countsForTenant interpolates tenantId into a postgres.js tagged template, which binds it as a $1 parameter rather than SQL text — pattern-matchers flag it but the library exonerates it. PASS.
